title: Commentary on Policy/Limits Compliance: December 31, 2026
content:
The credit union's compliance picture for the period ending March 31, 2026, is largely compliant. Asset quality metrics remain within limits; capital and solvency ratios meet targets with net worth growth on track; profitability measures show mixed results regarding expense control and return expectations; liquidity positions are generally stable though membership acquisition lags behind goals.

Asset Quality
Classified assets to net worth sit at 3%, well below the <40% threshold. Delinquent loans represent a negligible portion of total loans, while charge-offs remain zero across all categories. All asset quality indicators stay comfortably within their respective limits.

Capital & Solvency
The credit union maintains strong capital buffers with a net worth ratio at 10%, meeting the >=10% benchmark. Net worth growth recorded for the period was 7%, exceeding the target of 5%. The solvency evaluation stands at 110%, significantly above the minimum floor of 7%. Capital and solvency metrics are fully within their targets.

Profitability & Growth
Operating expenses as a percentage of average assets reached 4.0% in Q3, which is Above Limit against the <=3.50% guideline. Net operating expenses similarly hit 3.0%, breaching the ceiling set at <=3.0%. However, loan growth achieved 5%, falling within the 5%-10% range.

Interest Rate & Funding
Cost of funds remains low relative to average assets and stays well below the limit. The net interest margin sits comfortably above its target floor. Yield on loans is also performing favorably against benchmarks. Cash investments are maintained at a healthy level, though regular shares represent only 25% of total capital sources, which falls Below Target for the >=70% requirement.

Closing
The credit union demonstrates robust asset quality and solid solvency positions while managing funding costs effectively; however, elevated expense ratios relative to assets and lower-than-target membership growth require management focus. The institution will prioritize cost containment initiatives in its upcoming operational planning.
